lane: Lane 1 v3 — wider 4x8 VNNI register tile for the unified prefill owner
host: win i7-11800H (8C/16T Tiger Lake, AVX-512 + AVX512-VNNI; RTX 3060 6GB hidden)
what_v3_is: q8_0_packed_rows4_gemm4_accumulate_block_avx512vnni_4x8 + a paired loop nest: process TWO output groups per input load (8 output lanes), so each streamed input block is dpbusd'd against both weight bands. Wider AVX-512 register tile (llama uses 32 registers for bigger tiles). Bit-exact (each cell computed identically to the 4x4 path).

parity.kernel_bit_identity: PASS — unit test asserts to_bits()==baseline for BOTH 4x4 and 4x8 VNNI across rows {4,5,8,13,16} x roles {ffn_down, linear, attention_k}
parity.end_to_end_greedy: PASS — first_divergent == -1 (4x8 owner vs off) on Llama-3.2-3B-Q8

verdict: SUPERSEDED by q8-prefill-owner-hardened-20260628.json — the hardened in-process PAIRED sweep proves 4x8 is a SIGNIFICANT +3.3%/+3.8% win over 4x4 (CI excludes 1.0, 7-8/8 rounds), NOT a null. The null below was an artifact of the noisy cross-invocation method. ORIGINAL (kept for the record): HONEST NULL on this host. Same-build A/B (4x8 vs 4x4 VNNI, run consecutively, median-of-5) is INCONCLUSIVE — the box is thermally noisy after a long bench session (the SAME llama3b config swung 0.897x then 1.044x across two runs, a ~10% noise floor). Mean 4x8/4x4 ratio over 4 runs = 0.994 -> neutral within noise. The ~3% effect a 4x8 tile might give is not resolvable here. NOT a reproducible win; NOT promoted to the owner default.
decision: Default OFF — the owner ships the proven 4x4 VNNI tile (v2, +6%/+14%, reproducible). 4x8 kept as opt-in (CAMELID_X86_Q8_MATMUL_OWNER_4X8=1) so a thermally-stable host (e.g. the pending Ubuntu/server box) can re-evaluate whether the wider tile resolves a real gain there. Code is bit-exact + tested; only the default is conservative.
next_v3_real: If a stable host shows 4x8 helps, the bigger structural lever remains GotoBLAS-style 2D cache blocking (block the input dimension so both operands stay cache-resident) + cross-projection single-quant. Both bable on top of v2.
